title: What Have I Done

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Um... - Ah, you read the script. - I d... I did. - Well, congratulations. - You can read. That's so great.
jokes.setup: Larry's sarcastic response to being congratulated for reading a script
jokes.punchline: Treating basic literacy as an achievement worthy of congratulation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry sarcasm with perfect delivery - takes a mundane congratulation and turns it absurd

jokes.text: Yeah, I was calling to see if Jake had converted. Ba-dump-bump.
jokes.setup: Assistant gives Larry a rimshot sound after his Jewish conversion joke
jokes.punchline: The awkward 'ba-dump-bump' response treating Larry like a Catskills comedian
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of the ba-dump-bump, mildly amusing setup for later payoffs

jokes.text: maybe you can think of an adjective for the script
jokes.setup: After the assistant gives him a rimshot instead of feedback
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically suggesting she actually do her job and critique the script
jokes.explanation: Decent Larry snark but fairly predictable response

jokes.text: Yeah, like I'm a comic in the Catskills.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why the ba-dump-bump bothered him
jokes.punchline: The specific reference to Catskills comedy shows Larry's cultural specificity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry complaint with cultural specificity that feels authentic to the character

jokes.text: She's not a court stenographer.
jokes.setup: Jake saying his assistant takes notes on calls
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly specific comparison to court stenographers
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic - finding an oddly specific comparison to make his point

jokes.text: Should I be writing all this, Jake?
jokes.setup: Assistant asking if she should take notes on Larry telling her to get off
jokes.punchline: The meta-absurdity of documenting the argument about documentation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke with perfect timing - the absurdity of taking notes on being told not to take notes

jokes.text: You might wanna check your notes.
jokes.setup: Jake says he doesn't remember what Larry told him
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically referencing the note-taking he just objected to
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that uses Larry's earlier complaint as ammunition - very characteristic

jokes.text: You can just kiss goodbye to the fertilizer run up bill.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about the show without the right actor
jokes.punchline: The bizarre phrase 'fertilizer run up bill' as entertainment industry jargon
jokes.explanation: Weak joke - seems like nonsensical industry speak without clear comedic logic

jokes.text: One vote. Your fault.
jokes.setup: Irma apologizing to Larry about the election
jokes.punchline: Her blunt assignment of blame for losing by one vote
jokes.explanation: Brutal economy in just three words - perfectly captures the guilt and blame dynamic

jokes.text: I was watching the House of Representatives on C-SPAN, and it made me think of you.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to be romantic/apologetic
jokes.punchline: Comparing his romantic interest to congressional proceedings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry romantic gesture - completely tone-deaf but earnest in its absurdity

jokes.text: I bet Irma would be a great filibusteress.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing his political romantic metaphor
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'filibusteress' and treating it as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that extends the political metaphor - characteristic Larry awkwardness

jokes.text: But not tonight. I'm finishing a cleanse. I'm preparing for colonoscopy.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Irma to dinner
jokes.punchline: The immediate overshare about medical procedures
jokes.explanation: Standard overshare humor - gets a reaction but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: One vote.
jokes.setup: The colonoscopy conversation ending
jokes.punchline: Irma returning to the guilt trip with just two words
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing - the awkward pause filled with guilt. Maximum impact with minimum words

jokes.text: Carly likes that? - I kinda think it's adorable.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about the ba-dump-bump
jokes.punchline: Jeff finding it adorable, revealing his infatuation
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal moment - Jeff's obvious crush showing through his defense

jokes.text: because Carly smells like that. I'm with her, if Susie smells it on me, she'll get suspicious.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he's buying lilac body wash
jokes.punchline: The elaborate cover-up scheme for his affair
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff scheming - the convoluted logic of adultery planning

jokes.text: Isn't that how a line works? I don't like a big gap in the line.
jokes.setup: Larry noticing a woman not moving up in line
jokes.punchline: His obsessive analysis of proper line etiquette
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession - turning minor social observation into a philosophical position

jokes.text: you have a responsibility as a person on a line to keep the line moving. You're not holding up your end of the bargain.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining line etiquette to the woman
jokes.punchline: Treating line participation like a social contract
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - elevating mundane social interaction to contractual obligation level

jokes.text: Would you tell somebody that was a white man to just move up? - Yeah, in a second.
jokes.setup: Woman accusing Larry of racial bias
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate, confident response that he would
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Larry's oblivious certainty in the face of racial accusation

jokes.text: I tap her on the shoulder and you would've thought I called her the 'N' word.
jokes.setup: Larry recounting the line incident to Jeff
jokes.punchline: His tone-deaf comparison and use of 'the N word'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry tone-deafness - missing the point entirely while being technically correct

jokes.text: It is racist. - What? - Of course. - Get out of here. Only a Black person would tell another Black person, and they'd move the fuck up.
jokes.setup: Larry thinking his action was anti-racist
jokes.punchline: Leon's matter-of-fact explanation of racial dynamics
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leon wisdom - cutting through Larry's rationalization with street-smart racial reality

jokes.text: You out of your lane. You swerved out of your fucking lane. Stay in your lane. That's fucking racist.
jokes.setup: Larry arguing it's anti-racist
jokes.punchline: Leon's extended car metaphor for social boundaries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon metaphor - the repeated 'lane' imagery perfectly captures social boundaries

jokes.text: It's audacity. You had the fucking audacity.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining why Larry was wrong
jokes.punchline: The perfect word choice of 'audacity' delivered with conviction
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice - 'audacity' captures both the presumption and the nerve involved

jokes.text: You shouldn't have asked that lady to move up in your fucking white condition.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining racial dynamics
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'white condition' as a medical/social state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant phrase invention - 'white condition' as both identity and limitation is comedy gold

jokes.text: It's gotta be Black on Black. - So, bald on bald. - Bald on bald. - Jew on Jew. - Jew on Jew is cool too.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining social interaction rules
jokes.punchline: The extension to other identity categories with Larry's contributions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation building the social rules system - each addition gets funnier

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait. Where the fuck is other seven years goin' at?
jokes.setup: Plumber saying warranty is 3-10 years
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate suspicion about the missing warranty time
jokes.explanation: Good Larry suspicious moment - immediately spotting the logical gap in the warranty claim

jokes.text: What'd you say? I told him to stop fucking around. It's a 50-dollar valve.
jokes.setup: Leon talking to the plumber privately
jokes.punchline: The dramatic reduction from major repair to $50 fix
jokes.explanation: Great payoff - the absurd price difference reveals the scam perfectly

jokes.text: He was gonna fuck you with sandpaper, you understand?
jokes.setup: Leon explaining how Larry was being scammed
jokes.punchline: The vivid, uncomfortable metaphor for being overcharged
jokes.explanation: Memorable Leon metaphor - graphic and specific imagery for financial exploitation

jokes.text: You know what you are? You're... You're like a househusband.
jokes.setup: Larry appreciating Leon's intervention
jokes.punchline: The gender-role reversal comparison
jokes.explanation: Sets up the business idea nicely - good character observation about Leon's role

jokes.text: They need a motherfucker who knows a little bit about everything and a lot about nothing.
jokes.setup: Leon describing the house husband business
jokes.punchline: The perfect paradox of knowing 'a lot about nothing'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon wisdom - the paradoxical expertise description is both funny and insightful

jokes.text: Are you fucking her? No! Are you fucking her mother?
jokes.setup: Ted asking about Maria Sofia
jokes.punchline: The immediate escalation to asking about the mother
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke but fairly predictable progression

jokes.text: House husband? - Yes. - That's right.
jokes.setup: Larry calling Leon for the car repair situation
jokes.punchline: The immediate recognition and pride in the business name
jokes.explanation: Nice callback setup showing the business is already operational

jokes.text: Cheryl is beautiful, you a handsome-ass devil. He gon' take advantage of your ass.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining why he needs to accompany Larry
jokes.punchline: The assumption that attractiveness leads to being overcharged
jokes.explanation: Good Leon logic connecting physical appearance to business vulnerability

jokes.text: You get little bits of tomato on a piece of hard bread. They pile it up... You take a bite it all falls, they're very structurally unsound.
jokes.setup: Larry advising against the bruschetta
jokes.punchline: The engineering analysis of bruschetta construction flaws
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-analysis - treating food architecture like an engineering problem

jokes.text: That would just be a tomato sandwich.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for extra bread for bruschetta
jokes.punchline: Waiter's matter-of-fact reduction of the request
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan response that punctures Larry's elaborate solution

jokes.text: Market salad. He hates us. - I do not like him at all. - He hates me.
jokes.setup: Ordering from the waiter after the bruschetta incident
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid interpretation of the waiter's attitude
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry paranoia but not particularly strong execution

jokes.text: I don't know about great. Maybe good. Great, uh... you know, Gandhi was great, Churchill, great. Gorbachev, for my money, great. But Susie Greene, I don't know if anybody's ever described her as 'Great.'
jokes.setup: Deidre saying Susie Greene is great
jokes.punchline: Larry's historical comparison scale putting Susie below world leaders
jokes.explanation: Good Larry pedantry - applying historical greatness standards to casual compliments

jokes.text: He... he's an electrician who doesn't know how to fix electricity.
jokes.setup: Explaining problems with house repairs
jokes.punchline: The fundamental contradiction of incompetent expertise
jokes.explanation: Standard incompetence joke - clear but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: I cannot stand those March sisters. Boy, do they get on my nerves.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Little Women
jokes.punchline: Larry's visceral reaction to beloved literary characters
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's contrarian nature with classic literature

jokes.text: The giggling all the time, making jam, getting under the blanket and cozying up with Marmee.
jokes.setup: Explaining what annoys him about the March sisters
jokes.punchline: The specific, petty complaints about wholesome family activities
jokes.explanation: Good specific complaints - Larry finding fault with idealized family behavior

jokes.text: Is there no creative realm these sisters can't conquer?
jokes.setup: Listing the sisters' various talents
jokes.punchline: The sarcastic complaint about their well-roundedness
jokes.explanation: Nice sarcastic take on literary perfectionism - Larry resenting fictional achievement

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, God. No. - Yeah, see I told you. Look at that.
jokes.setup: Larry warned Irma about the bruschetta
jokes.punchline: The immediate structural collapse proving his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Larry's earlier engineering critique - vindication through food failure

jokes.text: It's a crazy law. Huh? It's a safety thing you... you... You know, you gotta put a fence around your pool. - Why? That doesn't make any...
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about pool fence law
jokes.punchline: His incredulous reaction to basic safety regulations
jokes.explanation: Sets up the pool fence obsession but not particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: I haven't had sex with anyone in 13 years. Just a little heads up, you know?
jokes.setup: Larry and Irma heading back to her place
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact announcement of sexual drought
jokes.explanation: Standard overshare humor - gets a reaction but predictable setup

jokes.text: I've laid fallow, it's like whistling into the Sahara up there.
jokes.setup: Continuing the sexual drought discussion
jokes.punchline: The agricultural and geographical metaphors for sexual inactivity
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor mixing farming and desert imagery for sexual dryness - well crafted

jokes.text: It's better than I expected. But I have low expectations.
jokes.setup: Larry's post-sex evaluation
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment with immediate qualification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry review - the damning with faint praise is beautifully executed

jokes.text: Can I borrow your toothbrush? Not so sure about that. Don't think that's a great idea.
jokes.setup: Irma asking to share toothbrush after sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate rejection of intimate hygiene sharing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's intimacy boundaries - realistic cringe

jokes.text: What have I done?
jokes.setup: Larry realizing the implications of sleeping with Irma
jokes.punchline: The title-drop moment of regret and realization
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry regret moment - the title drop works as both comedy and plot point

jokes.text: It's like a stretchy... It's... It's orlon. - No, it's cashmere. - It's cashmilon.
jokes.setup: Arguing about sweater material
jokes.punchline: Larry's invented blend word 'cashmilon'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay - the fabric portmanteau is decent but not exceptional

jokes.text: If it was pure, I wouldn't shvitz.
jokes.setup: Defending his sweater fabric assessment
jokes.punchline: Using Yiddish 'shvitz' as fabric quality test
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic using personal sweating as fabric analysis - culturally specific

jokes.text: I don't wanna fight with you, lover.
jokes.setup: Argument about the sweater escalating
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward attempt at romantic defusion
jokes.explanation: Awkward Larry romance attempt - the 'lover' feels forced and uncomfortable

jokes.text: Once you start voting, there's taxes, and... and... and jury duty, and old-ass warrants that pop up out of nowhere.
jokes.setup: Asked if he votes
jokes.punchline: Leon's paranoid but logical reasons for avoiding civic engagement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon paranoia - the escalating consequences reveal character depth and street smarts

jokes.text: I'm eating all this. I'm nervous, You know, it's been like... It was a big night for me and now I have gas.
jokes.setup: Irma's post-sex morning anxiety
jokes.punchline: The immediate shift from romantic to digestive concerns
jokes.explanation: Standard overshare humor - predictable bodily function comedy

jokes.text: Gotta tap that ass under duress? That's the worst. Have you ever tapped someone under duress?
jokes.setup: Leon commenting on Larry's situation with Irma
jokes.punchline: The casual discussion of sex as business transaction under pressure
jokes.explanation: Good Leon street wisdom - treating Larry's romantic manipulation as business strategy

jokes.text: My uncle passed away unexpectedly. So, I had to tap this cougar at this mortuary. Got a half off that coffin.
jokes.setup: Leon sharing his own 'duress' experience
jokes.punchline: The funeral home sexual transaction for discount casket
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - mixing death, sex, and commerce with perfect Leon logic

jokes.text: They call 'em midgets or dwarves. I stack two on top of each other, make 'em one whole women. Tap that ass.
jokes.setup: Misunderstanding about Little Women
jokes.punchline: Leon's literal interpretation and mathematical solution
jokes.explanation: Decent Leon misunderstanding - the mathematical approach to the confusion is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Listen to me, Larry. You're old and you're bald. I can get past that, but I don't think I can schtup somebody my daughter doesn't like.
jokes.setup: Irma explaining why they need to break up
jokes.punchline: The brutal honesty followed by the daughter-approval requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfectly brutal Larry takedown - the casual cruelty followed by familial conditions

jokes.text: That unpleasant bitch got you twisted the fuck up right now.
jokes.setup: Leon observing Larry's emotional state after Irma leaves
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment of the relationship's effect
jokes.explanation: Good Leon observation - cutting through romantic pretense with street truth

jokes.text: It's disgusting. - Love it. You don't like Lilac? I'm surprised at you. I really am.
jokes.setup: Susie commenting on Larry's new lilac scent
jokes.punchline: Larry's reverse psychology and mock disappointment
jokes.explanation: Decent character interplay but not particularly strong - setup for bigger payoff

jokes.text: What are you? You're fucking Irma Kostroski now? Well, that's a rather crude way of putting it. Do I make love to her? Yes, I do.
jokes.setup: Susie's blunt accusation
jokes.punchline: Larry's genteel correction and formal admission
jokes.explanation: Good Larry pretension - the linguistic correction while admitting to the affair

jokes.text: And he found out about the five-foot fence law. - He's extorting you? - Yeah. - That's why she's cast.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the Maria Sofia situation
jokes.punchline: The revelation that it's actual extortion, not just bad casting
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation moment - the casual admission of criminal behavior

jokes.text: Ba-dump-bump. - The fuck was that? - What? Where you get that expression from?
jokes.setup: Susie using the ba-dump-bump
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate recognition and suspicion
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff - Larry immediately connecting the dots about Jeff's affair

jokes.text: I can't believe how House Husband is catching on. - Really? - Yeah, man.
jokes.setup: Checking on Leon's business success
jokes.punchline: The rapid success of the absurd business concept
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but not particularly strong - moves plot forward

jokes.text: If it's not a matter of national security, there's no reason for it. - Well, if it's not a matter of national security, why can't I listen?
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about assistants listening to calls
jokes.punchline: The assistant turning his logic back on him
jokes.explanation: Nice logical trap - the assistant using Larry's own argument against him

jokes.text: Would you like it if I listened in to you and you friends when you're talking? - Well, that's really pervy.
jokes.setup: Larry making his privacy argument
jokes.punchline: Assistant calling his hypothetical behavior pervy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the argument - the 'pervy' accusation shifts the dynamic

jokes.text: All you need is these right here. See that? We call them anchors. Man, woman. See this? See that? What I need you to do is make love to that goddamn shelf, and hang it up.
jokes.setup: Contractor suggesting expensive wall work
jokes.punchline: Leon's sexual metaphor for simple shelf installation
jokes.explanation: Great Leon metaphor mixing technical instruction with sexual imagery - memorable phrasing

jokes.text: I thought it was lingerie. I got a little disappointed.
jokes.setup: Larry giving Cheryl the lilac body wash
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's honest disappointment about the gift
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - Cheryl's honest reaction to Larry's ulterior motive gift

jokes.text: I don't like anything unnatural near my lymph.
jokes.setup: Discussion about body wash ingredients
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's oddly specific health concern about lymph nodes
jokes.explanation: Mild Cheryl health obsession moment - the lymph specificity is character-appropriate

jokes.text: I know I give off this, like, a sex musk because I am active again.
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting she might need body wash
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's proud admission of sexual activity aroma
jokes.explanation: Standard overshare humor - Cheryl's sexual confidence creates awkwardness

jokes.text: I bruised my testicle getting off the peloton.
jokes.setup: Cheryl inviting Larry to join her in shower
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly specific injury excuse
jokes.explanation: Good specific injury excuse - the peloton detail makes it feel authentic and absurd

jokes.text: Your assistant, she... Told me it was at 6:00. - Britney? - She told me six o'clock. I don't... I think you must've taken that down wrong.
jokes.setup: Larry arriving early to the party
jokes.punchline: The immediate assumption that Larry got it wrong
jokes.explanation: Standard miscommunication setup - not particularly strong but moves plot

jokes.text: Britney wanted to listen in on the conversation, I got upset, so she gave me the wrong time.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's early
jokes.punchline: Larry's conspiracy theory about assistant revenge
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting earlier phone frustration to current predicament

jokes.text: My money, June Allyson. - Really? - With the bangs, cute. Cute. Cute? That's your criterion for what makes a great Jo?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to make amends about Little Women
jokes.punchline: His shallow criteria for judging literary adaptations
jokes.explanation: Good Larry shallow analysis - reducing literary performance to physical attractiveness

jokes.text: And, by the way, who doesn't love a good bang?
jokes.setup: Larry defending his preference for bangs
jokes.punchline: The unintentional double entendre
jokes.explanation: Obvious double entendre - predictable wordplay that doesn't rise above basic level

jokes.text: I can be a guest without a host. - Can you? - I... I think I can.
jokes.setup: Larry insisting he can stay while they get ready
jokes.punchline: The philosophical question of hostless guesting
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic problem - turning social etiquette into philosophical inquiry

jokes.text: You're tucking in the sweater? - Yes. How long you been doing that? - My whole life.
jokes.setup: Larry noticing the videographer's tucked sweater
jokes.punchline: Larry's fascination with this clothing choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obsession with minor details - the sweater-tucking fixation is character-appropriate

jokes.text: You're tucking in the sweater? - Yes. How long you been doing that? - My whole life. - Do people comment on it? There's almost never conversation about it.
jokes.setup: Larry noticing videographer's tucked sweater
jokes.punchline: Larry's fascination with rare tucking behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession - fixating on minor clothing detail with anthropological curiosity

jokes.text: I mean, the only person I've ever seen tuck is James Mason in Lolita. 'Lolita, do you think I should tuck in my sweater? Does it look good? What would you do?'
jokes.setup: Larry's sweater-tucking analysis
jokes.punchline: The specific movie reference with improvised dialogue
jokes.explanation: Great Larry cultural reference with improvised movie dialogue - specific and absurd

jokes.text: That's my favorite candle. Please, put that down. Why would you pick that up?
jokes.setup: Larry exploring their house unsupervised
jokes.punchline: Deidre's horror at Larry touching their belongings
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment - Larry's boundary violations creating discomfort

jokes.text: I don't know why he's not just sitting in one place and looking at his phone... like a normal person.
jokes.setup: Scott watching Larry explore their house
jokes.punchline: The modern definition of 'normal' guest behavior
jokes.explanation: Good modern observation about default social behavior - phone as social lubricant

jokes.text: I don't think I've ever seen anyone take, 'Make yourself at home' so literally.
jokes.setup: Larry adjusting their thermostat and exploring
jokes.punchline: The meta-observation about Larry's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-observation - perfectly captures Larry's literal-minded social violations

jokes.text: Oh, my God. He's taking his pants off. He is. - What is he doing? No. - Don't do that. Why is he tucking his sweater into his pants?
jokes.setup: Larry undressing and redressing in their house
jokes.punchline: The horror at watching someone tuck their sweater properly
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff - the sweater obsession leads to actual demonstration

jokes.text: Let's get out the anti-nausea pills. - I'll start... crying. - Oh, God.
jokes.setup: The couple beginning their vow renewal
jokes.punchline: Larry's visceral reaction to romantic displays
jokes.explanation: Good Larry reaction to romance - the physical nausea response is character-appropriate

jokes.text: I would rather masturbate in... in public than have to say that stuff.
jokes.setup: Listening to the romantic vows
jokes.punchline: Larry's extreme comparison for avoiding romantic expression
jokes.explanation: Strong Larry revulsion - the extreme comparison perfectly captures his romantic aversion

jokes.text: A mosquito got caught in my throat and I put my finger down there, and if they see the video... it might be, you know, misconstrued.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get the videographer to erase footage
jokes.punchline: His absurd excuse for what was clearly inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry excuse-making - the elaborate mosquito story for obvious misbehavior

jokes.text: Samuel Tannenbaum? - Yes. - Samuel, my Hebrew brother. Could you be a mensch and maybe reconsider this position for a... fellow tribesman.
jokes.setup: Larry needing the video erased
jokes.punchline: Playing the Jewish card with elaborate cultural appeals
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Leon's advice - Larry deploying the Jewish card with full cultural language

jokes.text: I define it as, 'Netflix and chilling with you.'
jokes.setup: Scott's wedding vows about paradise
jokes.punchline: Mixing romantic sentiment with modern slang
jokes.explanation: Weak attempt at modern romance - the Netflix reference feels forced and cliché

jokes.text: Irma Kostroski! Keep your fucking hands off of my husband, you homewrecker! You whore! You slut!
jokes.setup: Susie arriving at the party and seeing Irma
jokes.punchline: The explosive confrontation ruining the anniversary
jokes.explanation: Great climactic payoff - all plot threads converging in public disaster

jokes.text: I smell the lilacs, baby. I smell the lilacs and I knew it was you.
jokes.setup: Susie's accusation
jokes.punchline: Scent-based detective work revealing the affair
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Jeff's elaborate scent plan backfires spectacularly through Susie's detection

jokes.text: I got a whole fucking deck of cards. I... I got the Black card, I got the handsome card, the tall card, the Big Johnson card...
jokes.setup: Leon explaining card theory success
jokes.punchline: Leon's comprehensive inventory of social advantages
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leon expansion of card theory - perfectly confident self-assessment with escalating claims

jokes.text: I got the Black card, I got the handsome card, the tall card, the Big Johnson card... - Oh, hold on. Hold it. Hold it. Handsome card? - Fuck, yeah. - You don't have a handsome card.
jokes.setup: Leon listing his advantages
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately challenging the handsome claim
jokes.explanation: Good callback extension with Larry's immediate reality check on Leon's self-assessment

jokes.text: Pickles? Ah! The brine. That's what it is. - She stinks from pickles.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing why Irma smells bad
jokes.punchline: The pickle brine revelation solving the mystery
jokes.explanation: Good detective moment - Larry solving the aromatic mystery with typical bluntness

jokes.text: Hiding a second fucking phone, you asshole. Are you kidding me? Really, Jeff? Really? You stupid moron, you are fucking caught, asshole. Gotcha.
jokes.setup: Susie discovering Jeff's burner phone
jokes.punchline: Her triumphant discovery speech
jokes.explanation: Standard discovery moment - Susie's fury is character-appropriate but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: Take this down. She's fucking my husband. 'Ba-dump-bump.'
jokes.setup: Susie calling Jake about the Irma situation
jokes.punchline: Using the ba-dump-bump as punctuation for the accusation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback finale - the ba-dump-bump becomes a weapon of revenge with perfect timing

tmdb_overview: Larry does damage control to remain in Irma's good graces while encouraging Leon to monetize his knack for husbandly counsel.
